summary: No hardcoded model literal is present, but the plan route does not actually enforce the router-selected target and it fails open when routing cannot be resolved, so Decision 5 is not reliably satisfied.

findings.title: Resolved router target is never sent to the gateway, so the actual planning model remains whatever the gateway re-classifies
findings.recommendation: Make the route selection binding: send an explicit routing hint/classification or target to the gateway and reject the request if the gateway cannot honor it. As written, `resolveRoute()` only populates `routedVia`; the POST body carries only `{ message: prompt }`, so the gateway is free to classify the prompt again and pick a different target/model than the one this route computed.

findings.title: Routing failures are swallowed and planning proceeds through a fallback path instead of failing closed
findings.recommendation: Treat router resolution/config errors as request failures for this endpoint, or at minimum require a successfully resolved route before invoking the gateway. The current `catch {}` path leaves `routedVia = "default"` and still executes the prompt, which violates the requirement that this call must route through the Model Router.
